content:
First note that for any \(a<391\) we have
\[
\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)\;>\;\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\frac{ab}{\gcd(a,b)}>\frac{a\cdot 391}{\gcd(a,391)}
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
b\;\gcd(a,391)\;>\;391\,\gcd(a,b).
\]
Write \(g_1=\gcd(a,391)\in\{1,17,23\}\), \(g_2=\gcd(a,b)\).  Then we need
\[
b\;>\;\frac{391\,g_2}{g_1}.
\]
To make the right‐hand side as small as possible, we take \(g_1=23\) (so \(a\) is a multiple of 23 but not of 17) and \(g_2=1\) (so \(\gcd(a,b)=1\)).  Then the condition is
\[
b>\frac{391\cdot1}{23}=17,
\]
so the smallest integer \(b\) that works is \(b=18\).  For example, with \(a=23\) and \(b=18\) one checks
\[
\lcm(23,18)=414>391=\lcm(23,391),
\]
so \((23,18)\) is a “pupusa,” and \(b=18\) is minimal.  Hence the answer is
\[
\boxed{18}.
\]

\[

\]
